user_query:
I'm the staff engineer leading our team's move to standardized Swagger across all services. Please read these inputs and produce a complete, team-usable "Swagger Adoption Pack" tailored to our organization:

Inputs to read:
- input/company_profile.json (company name, compliance profile, team context)
- input/api_inventory.csv (service inventory: service, owner, has_swagger, lang, priority)
- input/constraints.yaml (technical constraints and preferences)

Deliverables to write:
1) output/swagger_adoption/guide.md
   Requirements:
   - Start with an H1: "Swagger Adoption Guide"
   - Include the following exact sections as H2s in this order: "Overview", "Quickstart", "Patterns & Anti-Patterns", "Security", "Performance", "Migration Plan", "Debugging", "Cheatsheet"
   - Tailor the content to our company profile; include the company name from input/company_profile.json in the Overview
   - Under Security, explicitly call out: role-based access control and encryption in transit and at rest
   - Add a Markdown table that summarizes every service from input/api_inventory.csv with columns: Service, Owner, Swagger Status (Present/Absent), Language, Priority, Next Action. Use the has_swagger field to set Present/Absent, and propose a practical Next Action per service
   - In Quickstart, provide a step-by-step checklist for teams to get a spec created and reviewed
   - In Migration Plan, include a 30/60/90-day rollout with checkpoints
   - In Cheatsheet, provide 5–10 quick-reference tips for working with Swagger in dev workflows

2) output/swagger_adoption/policy.yaml
   Requirements:
   - Valid YAML that can be parsed
   - Include keys with the following minimum structure and values:
     security:
       authorization: role_based_access_control
       encryption: [in_transit, at_rest]
     versioning: semver
     review: ["spec review", "code quality review"]
     style_guide:
       - no hardcoded credentials
       - document all custom configurations
   - You may add other sensible keys if needed, but keep these exact values present

3) output/swagger_adoption/checklist.json
   Requirements:
   - Valid JSON array of at least 8 items
   - Each item an object with fields: id (string), title (string), status (one of: "todo", "in_progress", "done")
   - Include at least one task each that clearly targets: security, performance, migration, and debugging (case-insensitive keyword match is fine)

General:
- Keep everything in English
- Do not use placeholders—tailor to the provided inputs
- Ensure the YAML and JSON files are well-formed and parseable
- Write only to the paths listed above

Acceptance checklist (must pass):
- guide.md exists and contains the specified H2 sections and the company name
- The services table lists all services from input/api_inventory.csv and uses Present/Absent correctly
- policy.yaml parses and contains the required keys/values exactly as specified
- checklist.json parses, has ≥ 8 items, each with id/title/status, and includes tasks covering security, performance, migration, and debugging
